hero.title: Built to sell.
hero.problem: Most Cairo businesses have sites that look credible and convert nothing. We engineer custom Next.js systems - no templates, no shortcuts - that load fast, rank well, and turn visitors into customers.
hero.description: You own everything. No templates, no lock-in.
hero.trust: On time · Zero templates · Full code ownership

process.title: Zero ambiguity.
process.subtitle: Four phases. Every deliverable defined upfront. No surprises in scope, timeline, or ownership.

step1.title: Discovery & Diagnosis
step1.description: We audit your systems, map your acquisition flow, and locate where performance is bleeding. Every decision starts with evidence.
step1.deliverables: Technical audit report, Architecture brief, Priority matrix

step2.title: Architecture & Specification
step2.description: We design the full system before writing a line of code. Data models, API contracts, component hierarchy - all locked down before build begins.
step2.deliverables: System design doc, Component spec, Tech stack decision

step3.title: Engineering & Build
step3.description: Frontend, backend, integrations - built against the spec. No shortcuts that create problems six months from now.
step3.deliverables: Production codebase, Staging environment, QA report

step4.title: Launch & Handover
step4.description: Deployment, performance benchmarking, and a structured handover. You receive full ownership: documentation, credentials, autonomy.

hero.title: How we work.
hero.description: A framework, not a formula. Every project follows the same backbone - adapted to what it actually needs.

discovery.title: Discovery
discovery.description: We listen before we build. 60 minutes. No sales pitch - just the right questions.
discovery.deliverables: Requirements summary | Technical audit | Project roadmap

wireframe.title: Wireframe
wireframe.description: You approve the structure before a single pixel is designed.

design.title: Design
design.description: You see the full design before we write any code. Revisions included.
design.deliverables: Visual mockups | Responsive layouts | Brand alignment

development.title: Development
development.description: We build. You track live progress via a staging link - no black boxes.
development.deliverables: Working site | Live preview link | Weekly updates

launch.title: Launch
launch.description: You receive everything: source code, hosting setup, documentation. Zero lock-in.

flexibility.title: The process serves the project.
flexibility.description: These phases are a framework, not a rigid contract. Some projects need deeper discovery. Others need to ship fast. We adjust the intensity - never the discipline.

closing.cta: Ready to begin? Book a Discovery Call

socialProof.title: What changed after launch.
socialProof.subtitle: From the founders and directors who needed more than a website.

art-lighting-store-founder.quote: Our old store made it impossible for customers to see why our fixtures were worth the price. The new system changed that - conversion is up and support tickets dropped.
art-lighting-store-founder.author: Founder, Art Lighting Store

casescobra-owner.quote: The configurator Altruvex built finally matches how our customers actually shop. Average order value climbed, and the 'this isn't what I expected' messages nearly disappeared.

newlight-commercial-director.quote: For the first time, our Arabic and English presence feels equally considered. The site is fast, stable, and our sales team uses it in front of clients.

comparisonDemo.title: Generic systems cost you
comparisonDemo.subtitle: Every template site is built for the vendor's convenience — not your conversions. Here's what that actually looks like in numbers.

optimized.headline: Custom web systems that
optimized.subtext: Next.js development in Arabic and English. No templates. Measurable results.

generic.issue1: Slow load — visitors leave before page renders
generic.issue2: No RTL — Arabic audience bounces immediately
generic.issue3: Template lock-in — can't grow past the theme

comparisonDemo.metricsFooterGeneric: Industry average for generic template-based sites.
comparisonDemo.metricsFooterOptimized: Based on real client results — avg. 90-day period.
comparisonDemo.contextBodyGeneric: One-size-fits-all. Built for the vendor, not your users.
comparisonDemo.contextBodyOptimized: Engineered for your audience, stack, and conversion goals.
comparisonDemo.footerNote: Results vary by project — these are real averages.

problem.subtitle: Most businesses lose leads before the conversation starts. Here's why:

templates.title: Template Trap
templates.description: Your site looks identical to your competitor's because you both bought the same $60 theme. Visitors have no reason to choose you.

speed.title: 3-Second Rule
speed.description: 53% of mobile users leave if a page takes longer than 3 seconds to load. Slow sites don't lose rankings - they lose customers.

confusion.title: Unclear Value
confusion.description: Visitors land on your page and can't tell what you do, who it's for, or why they should care. Confusion doesn't convert.

amateurs.title: Silent Failures
amateurs.description: Broken links, janky scroll, contact forms that fail quietly - without ever notifying you that a lead just disappeared.

work.title: Three builds.
work.subtitle: Performance numbers, technical decisions, and what changed after launch - not screenshots.
work.description: Three projects that show how we think about performance, bilingual UX, and conversion - with the metrics to back it up.

services.title: Four systems. One standard.
services.subtitle: Every layer engineered precisely - from the interface to the data model.

service1.title: Custom Web Applications
service1.description: React/Next.js systems built to onboard users, reduce churn, and grow revenue - not just to look good on a screen.

service2.title: E-Commerce That Converts
service2.description: Custom storefronts engineered for speed and revenue. Every step from product page to checkout optimized to reduce abandonment and raise order value.

service3.title: Bilingual Business Portals
service3.description: Arabic (RTL) and English (LTR) built as equals - not a translation of one into the other. Every component reconsidered for both directions.

service4.title: Performance Rescues
service4.description: Your current site is slow, broken, or failing to convert. We audit, diagnose, and rebuild what needs rebuilding - without starting from scratch.

about.title: One team.
about.description1: Altruvex is a custom web development studio in Cairo. We build modern web systems for businesses that need quality architecture - not template shortcuts.
about.description2: Every project gets our full attention. No outsourcing. No templates. No junior devs handed the keys. Clean code, honest timelines, full transparency throughout.
about.founderNote: You work directly with the founder - no handoffs, no account managers between you and the engineer.

cta.title: Stress-test your idea before you build it.
cta.description: We help founders and teams clarify technical direction, cut build risk, and design systems that scale - before a single line of code is committed.
cta.footnote: No sales pitch. No pressure. A direct technical conversation.

hero.title: Systems that outlast trends.
hero.description: We build to last. The difference isn't the framework - it's the questions asked before the first commit.

1.common: Build the features users asked for
1.altruvex: Understand the problem users are actually trying to solve

2.altruvex: Define architectural constraints before opening the editor

3.altruvex: Launch is when the system starts working - not when fixes start

data.title: Data First
data.description: Before UI, before features, before brand. We map what the system needs to know. A weak data model cannot be saved by good code.

scale.title: Design for 10x
scale.description: Not because you'll get there - but because growth assumptions change every technical decision you make.

maintenance.title: Minimize Intervention Cost
maintenance.description: A system that needs constant attention isn't finished. We optimize for long stretches of stability - where nothing breaks, nothing drifts.

handoff.title: Write for the Next Engineer
handoff.description: Code written for one person is fragile. Every commit assumes someone else will read and extend it in six months.

constraints.title: Constraints are a design tool.
constraints.paragraphs:
Unlimited options create paralysis, not products. We start every project by defining what we won't build - which features are out of scope, which use cases won't be supported, which browsers won't be accommodated.

This isn't limitation. It's focus. A system designed for everyone serves no one well.

Constraints force decisions. Decisions create character. Character builds a product with a point of view.

bilingual.title: Structure that respects direction.
bilingual.paragraphs:
Most sites treat Arabic RTL as an afterthought - flip the layout and hope it works. We design systems where direction is a first-class architectural decision from day one.

This isn't about translation. It's about cultural adaptation. Right-aligned navigation isn't mirrored - it's reconsidered.

Every component, every layout, every interaction - built to perform in both directions without compromise.

items.1: Accept projects with fixed deadlines set before the scope is understood
items.2: Build systems we can't stand behind technically
items.3: Work through agencies that sit between us and the end client
items.5: Start work before the problem is fully understood

closing.title: If this matches how you think,
closing.description: Not every project needs this level of thinking. Some need speed. Some need iteration. But if you're building something that needs to last - if the system is the product, not the storefront - we should talk. No pitch. No pressure. A direct technical discussion about what you're building and whether we're the right fit.

hero.title: Every project clears these benchmarks.
hero.description: We build digital systems designed for growth, performance, and long-term ownership. These are non-negotiable.

webVitals.label: Core Web Vitals
webVitals.description: FCP < 1.5s · TTI < 3.5s · CLS < 0.1 on primary pages.

code.title: Clean, Maintainable Code
code.description: Code is read more often than it's written. We optimize for clarity, consistency, and maintainability - not clever shortcuts.
code.requirements: Strict TypeScript | Component-based architecture | Automated testing | Continuous integration
code.benchmarks: Zero lint errors | > 80% test coverage | Documented API endpoints

performance.title: Speed as a Revenue Driver
performance.description: Every 100ms of latency costs conversion. Performance is a design constraint from day one - not an optimization pass.
performance.requirements: Edge-first delivery | Modern image formats | Minimal JS execution | Font optimization

accessibility.title: Accessible by Default
accessibility.description: If it isn't accessible, it isn't finished. WCAG compliance is a build requirement, not a post-launch checklist.
accessibility.requirements: Semantic HTML | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance | Keyboard accessibility | Screen reader support
accessibility.benchmarks: Lighthouse Accessibility > 95 | No ARIA errors | Contrast ratio > 4.5:1

security.title: Security from Day One
security.description: Trust is built through security baked in - not bolted on. CSP headers, dependency audits, and input sanitization before launch.
security.requirements: CSP headers | Secure authentication | Sanitized inputs | Dependency audits
security.benchmarks: Grade A security headers | No high-risk CVEs | Regular patch cycles

enforcement.title: Accountability built in.
enforcement.description: CI/CD pipelines block deployments if these standards aren't met. Quality doesn't degrade over time - it's enforced on every deploy.

hero.title: Technical writing.
hero.description: Decisions, trade-offs, and systems - examined in depth. Not tutorials. Perspectives from building real products.

why-not-wordpress.title: Why we don't build on WordPress
why-not-wordpress.excerpt: WordPress runs 43% of the web. We're not part of that 43%. Here's exactly why technical debt compounds faster on WordPress than any other platform - and what the alternative actually looks like.

technical-debt.title: The real cost of technical debt
technical-debt.excerpt: Every shortcut taken today is a loan against tomorrow's engineering velocity. Most teams don't realize they're insolvent until a rewrite is the only option.

evaluating-developers.title: How to evaluate a web development partner
evaluating-developers.excerpt: Agencies promise the world. Freelancers promise speed. In-house promises control. All three are often wrong. Here's what separates a good technical partner from an expensive mistake.

multilingual-architecture.title: Architecting multilingual web systems
multilingual-architecture.excerpt: Most 'bilingual' websites are English sites with Arabic text dropped in. Real multilingual architecture means rethinking layout, data structures, and content strategy from the foundation up.

footer.tagline: Custom web systems. Built to outlast the next trend.
footer.description: Altruvex is a custom web development studio in Cairo. We build React/Next.js systems for businesses that need speed, structure, and zero vendor lock-in.

servicesPage.title: Four systems.
servicesPage.description: We build e-commerce stores that convert, web applications that scale, bilingual portals that serve Arabic and English speakers equally, and performance rescues for sites that have stopped working.
servicesPage.gridSubtitle: Every engagement scoped precisely. You pay for what your system actually needs.

webDesign.title: Interface Design
webDesign.description: Every layout, CTA, and flow designed to move users toward a decision - not just to look polished.

development.title: Custom Development
development.description: React/Next.js engineering with typed code, automated testing, and architecture your next developer can understand and extend.

consulting.title: Technical Strategy
consulting.description: Build the right system before you build the wrong one. We map constraints, define architecture, and help you make the decisions that matter most.

maintenance.title: System Maintenance
maintenance.description: Proactive monitoring, security patches, and performance upkeep - so your system keeps working long after launch day.

pipeline.title: How we build
pipeline.description: Every technical decision in our pipeline is geared towards long-term stability and performance.

serviceDetails.whatWeOfferSubtitle: Scoped to what your system needs - nothing more.

webDesign.title: Interface Design
webDesign.subtitle: Conversion-First Interface Design
webDesign.description: We design interfaces that move users toward decisions - not interfaces that look good in a Dribbble screenshot. Every layout, color, and CTA placement is deliberate.
webDesign.showcaseSubtitle: From e-commerce to corporate portals - every screen built with conversion and clarity as the brief.
webDesign.compareSubtitle: Drag the divider - same copy, same sections, opposite outcomes.

01.title: Conversion-First Layouts
01.description: Every page structure guides the visitor toward a specific action - not just presenting information.

02.title: User Flow Architecture
02.description: We map user journeys before designing screens, so every interface decision is grounded in how people actually navigate.

03.title: Precise Visual Hierarchy
03.description: Typography, spacing, and contrast calibrated so the most important element always wins the eye first.

04.title: Brand-System Alignment
04.description: Design that extends your brand identity into a consistent system - not a one-off visual that drifts after launch.

05.title: Performance-Aware Design
05.description: No bloated assets or animation for its own sake. Every visual choice is weighed against its load cost.

06.title: Accessibility Compliant
06.description: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance built into the design system - not patched in after development ends.

cta.title: Need an interface that converts?
cta.description: We'll review your current design and scope what needs to change - in a free 30-minute call.

development.title: Custom Development
development.subtitle: React / Next.js Engineering
development.description: Production-grade web systems built with typed code, tested critical paths, and architecture designed for the next developer - not the next sprint.

01.title: React / Next.js Engineering
01.description: Full-stack builds on the stack that leads in performance, SEO, and developer experience - no templating shortcuts.

02.title: Scalable Architecture
02.description: Systems designed for 10x traffic and feature scope before you need it. Architecture decisions made upfront, not retrofitted under pressure.

03.title: API & Integration Layer
03.description: RESTful and GraphQL APIs built with clear contracts, error handling, and documentation your team can use.

04.title: Data Model Design
04.description: Database schemas that won't become technical debt in 18 months. We design data structures before we design screens.

05.title: Automated Testing
05.description: Critical user flows covered by automated tests. No deployment goes live without passing CI gates.

06.title: Post-Launch Support
06.description: The system doesn't stop at launch day. We monitor, patch, and support the production environment throughout the agreed period.

techStack.title: Deliberate Technology Choices
techStack.subtitle: No trend-chasing. Every dependency earns its place.
techStack.diagramLabel: Service architecture diagram - select a node to inspect dependencies
techStack.inspect: Select a technology node to inspect

nextjs.description: Full-stack React framework powering every project. App Router, server components, ISR, and edge runtime in one coherent system.

react.description: Component-based UI layer. Every interface is composable, reusable, and predictably state-driven with concurrent rendering.

typescript.description: Strict type safety across the entire codebase - APIs, database queries, and UI components all share a single type system.

nodejs.description: Server runtime for API routes, background jobs, webhooks, and serverless functions. Non-blocking I/O at the core.

postgresql.description: Production-grade relational database. ACID compliant, with JSON column support, full-text search, and row-level security.

prisma.description: Type-safe database client - schema is the source of truth, TypeScript types are generated automatically. No raw SQL drift.

tailwind.description: Utility-first CSS with design tokens baked in. Fast iteration, zero dead styles in production builds, and true dark mode.

vercel.description: Zero-config deployment with a global edge network, real-time analytics, instant rollbacks, and preview environments per branch.

vps.description: Powerful infrastructure for high-traffic platforms. We use custom VPS servers for complex projects requiring maximum performance, dedicated resources, and specialized configurations that go beyond standard hosting.

cta.title: Ready to build on a foundation that lasts?
cta.description: Let's scope the system architecture before any code is written.

consulting.title: Technical Strategy
consulting.subtitle: Architecture & Decision Consulting
consulting.description: Make the right technical decisions before you spend six months on the wrong system. We map your constraints, evaluate your options, and give you a clear build direction.

brief.title: The brief, read
brief.subtitle: Every annotation is a decision. No recommendation before the architecture is understood.

body.p1: We've been growing fast over the past 18 months, but <align>we can't seem to keep the team aligned.</align> Every sprint we're shipping things, but <picture>nothing feels connected to a bigger picture.</picture>
body.p2: <tooling>Our main problem is we need better tooling.</tooling> If we just had the right tools, the team would work better and we'd finally be able to scale.

value.title: Wrong builds cost more than right ones.
value.body1: Every recommendation is grounded in your specific business model, market, and constraints - not a playbook recycled from the last client.
value.body2: We help you make the right technical decisions today, and build the clarity to keep making them without us tomorrow.

approach.title: How We Work
approach.subtitle: A structured path from problem to decision

01.title: Understand
01.description: Map your current system, team constraints, and the actual problem you're trying to solve.

02.title: Diagnose
02.description: Identify where the current architecture, stack, or process is creating risk or limiting growth.

03.title: Recommend
03.description: A clear, opinionated technical direction with trade-offs spelled out honestly.

04.title: Transfer
04.description: Documentation and a handover that lets your team execute confidently - with or without us.

01.title: Technical Architecture Review
01.description: We evaluate your current or planned system and identify the decisions with the highest long-term consequence.

02.title: Performance Audit
02.description: A Lighthouse and Core Web Vitals deep-dive, with fixes ranked by revenue impact.

03.title: Build-vs-Buy Analysis
03.description: We tell you what to build custom, what to buy off-the-shelf, and where the line is - so you stop paying for complexity you don't need.

04.title: Code Quality Review
04.description: A structured review of your existing codebase with honest findings on maintainability, security exposure, and refactor priority.

05.title: Security Assessment
05.description: Systematic review of your authentication, input handling, headers, and dependencies - before a problem surfaces in production.

06.title: Team Knowledge Transfer
06.description: Structured sessions that leave your internal team more capable, not more dependent.

cta.title: Not sure which direction to build?
cta.description: Spend 30 minutes mapping your constraints. You'll leave with a clear technical direction - no commitment required.

maintenance.title: System Maintenance
maintenance.subtitle: Proactive System Support
maintenance.description: Most sites degrade silently after launch - security patches skipped, performance drifting, dependencies aging. We prevent that.

stats.title: Stability is an engineering decision.
stats.body1: Maintenance isn't reactive patching - it's the ongoing discipline that keeps a system performing the way it was built to perform.
stats.body2: We handle the technical layer so you can grow your business without being anchored by infrastructure that's quietly falling behind.

01.title: Proactive Monitoring
01.description: Issues detected and resolved before your users encounter them - not after a support ticket arrives.

02.title: Security Patching
02.description: Dependency updates, CVE monitoring, and security hardening on a regular cycle - not when it's already too late.

03.title: Performance Upkeep
03.description: Regular Core Web Vitals checks and bundle analysis to prevent the gradual performance drift that hits conversion.

04.title: Content & Feature Updates
04.description: Fast turnaround on copy changes, minor feature additions, and configuration updates - without a new project engagement.

05.title: Automated Backups
05.description: Daily encrypted backups with a tested recovery process. Not a checkbox - something that works when you need it.

06.title: Priority Support Channel
06.description: Direct access to the engineering team for critical issues - with guaranteed response times that match the severity.

pricing.title: Support Plans
pricing.subtitle: Choose the coverage your system needs

professional.name: Professional
features: Weekly updates and priority incident handling
Content and landing page updates included

enterprise.name: Enterprise
features: Dedicated success manager and custom SLAs

cta.title: Your system generates revenue. Protect it.
cta.description: Most critical failures are predictable. Let's set up the coverage that prevents them before they cost you.

estimator.title: Scope your project in 3 minutes.
estimator.subtitle: Answer a few questions and get a calibrated investment range based on the class of system you need.
estimator.description: No forms, no sales calls required to get a baseline number. Three questions, one honest range.

complete.title: Complete
complete.description: Full identity system ready: logo, colors, typography, and usage rules. We plug it in - no extra design scope needed.

partial.title: Partial
partial.description: You have a logo or basic visuals but no full system. We refine and extend what exists without starting from zero.

scratch.title: Starting Fresh
scratch.description: No brand identity yet. We build the full system - logo, colors, typography - as part of the project scope.

provide.title: Ready to Hand Over
provide.description: Copy and assets are structured and ready. We focus on architecture, design, and engineering.

need-help.title: Need Content Help
need-help.description: We co-create messaging and structure with you, which adds time to the early phases.

unsure.title: Not Defined Yet
unsure.description: Content isn't structured yet. We'll clarify key messages before locking timelines and scope.

deadline.title: When do you need to launch?

flexible.title: Flexible
flexible.description: 3+ months out. We optimize for depth, polish, and long-term maintainability.

2months.title: Within 2 Months
2months.description: ~8 weeks from kickoff. Scope kept tight to protect quality without rushing.

1month.title: Within 1 Month
1month.description: ~4 weeks. We scope a focused, shippable version of the system for this window.

urgent.title: As Soon as Possible
urgent.description: Under 4 weeks. Lean scope, high engineering intensity. Quality is non-negotiable.

website.title: Corporate Website
website.description: A high-performance marketing site built for clarity, trust, and lead generation - no app logic.

webapp.title: Custom Web Application
webapp.description: A product or internal tool with accounts, workflows, and logic - SaaS, dashboards, portals.

ecommerce.title: E-Commerce System
ecommerce.description: An online store with products, checkout, payments, and the inventory systems needed to reliably sell.

pwa.title: Progressive Web App
pwa.description: App-like mobile experience built on the web stack - offline support, installable, push notifications.

complexity.title: What scope level fits your needs?

basic.title: Focused
basic.description: Core features only. Straightforward flows, minimal custom logic. The right choice for a focused first release.

standard.title: Standard
standard.description: Additional workflows, integrations, and richer UI. Everything in Focused, plus more depth where it matters.

premium.title: Full Depth
premium.description: Advanced logic, multiple roles, bespoke interactions. For flagship products and complex systems.

timeline.title: What delivery pace do you need?

urgent.title: Sprint
urgent.description: Compressed, high-intensity build. Scope controlled tightly to maintain quality at speed.

standard.title: Balanced
standard.description: Structured cadence with enough time for discovery, design, build, and QA - no artificial pressure.

flexible.title: Extended
flexible.description: Extra time for iteration, testing, and refinement. Best for systems where quality matters more than speed.

medium.title: Connected System
medium.range: 150,000 – 450,000 EGP - systems with integrations, auth, or more advanced flows.

large.title: Full Infrastructure
large.range: 450,000+ EGP - full, multi-layer systems with infrastructure and internal tools.

custom.title: Architecture Consultation
custom.range: Custom Scope - we scope and price a bespoke architecture engagement.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about our process and pricing.
faq.q1: Why custom code instead of WordPress or Shopify?
faq.a1: Custom development gives you complete ownership, no platform constraints, and fundamentally better performance. A system built for your business logic loads faster, ranks higher, and converts more visitors than any plugin-dependent platform can.
faq.a2: This estimator is based on historical data from past projects. The range aligns closely with our typical engagements. Final numbers are confirmed after a detailed discovery session where we map your exact requirements.
faq.a3: Yes. All enterprise and large-scale projects include optional retainer packages for maintenance, feature development, and priority support. Every project also includes a 30-day post-launch warranty for critical fixes.
faq.q4: What happens after I request the PDF?
faq.a4: You receive your personalized estimate PDF immediately. We'll follow up within 24 hours to schedule a discovery call and walk through your requirements in detail.

offline.title: You're Offline
offline.description: It looks like you've lost your internet connection.
offline.description2: Check your network and try again.

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.title: Scoped to what you actually need.
pricing.description: Each tier is a different class of system - not a different page count. We scope precisely before anything is built, so you never pay for complexity your project doesn't require.

faq.title: Pricing Questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about our investment model and how we scope projects.

01.q: How do you determine the investment range?
01.a: We price by the class of system, not the page count. Underlying architecture, data complexity, and integration requirements drive the number - not arbitrary metrics like 'number of pages' or 'estimated hours.'

02.a: No. Year 1 includes domain registration, production hosting, SSL, and CDN. From Year 2, you pay a transparent infrastructure renewal fee. Optional maintenance retainers are agreed separately and always opt-in.

03.q: What does the payment schedule look like?
03.a: Milestone-based: a commencement deposit to secure the build window, then payments tied to discovery completion, design approval, and development delivery.

04.q: Can I upgrade my system tier later?
04.a: Yes. Every project is built on modular architecture so it can grow. Start with a Static System and layer on authentication, payments, or dashboards as your business evolves.

05.q: Do you work with early-stage startups?
05.a: Yes. Our Static System tier is designed for founders who need a flagship-quality web presence at a fixed, predictable cost. For more complex needs, we can phase the build to align with funding stages.

essential.name: Static System
essential.idealFor: Businesses that need a fast, conversion-focused web presence - no external services, no moving parts.
features: Custom architecture - no templates, no page builders
Next.js static or SSG build, production-optimized
Core Web Vitals - target 95+ across all metrics
Full handoff: source code, docs, deployment guide
essential.notIncluded: No user accounts, payment processing, or external API connections - see Connected System for those.

professional.name: Connected System
professional.idealFor: Platforms that need to connect to the world - payment gateways, CRMs, authentication, or any third-party service your business depends on.
features: Third-party API connections with error handling
Technical architecture session before build starts
professional.notIncluded: No automation pipelines or infrastructure management - see Full Infrastructure for those.

flagship.name: Full Infrastructure
flagship.idealFor: Organizations that need a complete technical backbone - not a website, but the operational infrastructure behind a growing business.
features: Infrastructure setup: hosting, CI/CD, domain management
Dedicated communication channel throughout the build

roi.title: The wrong system compounds in cost.
roi.calculation: A site rebuilt 18 months after launch because it couldn't handle integrations, user accounts, or traffic typically costs 2–3× the original build - plus the revenue lost while you waited.
roi.question: The real question isn't what this costs to build. It's what the wrong build costs over two years.

architecture.title: Architecture first. Execution second.
architecture.p1: We start with constraints and data before discussing pages or pixels. This is how we avoid rework and ship systems that outlast the current framework trend.
architecture.p2: Every project begins with a discovery session, a technical audit where needed, and a precise definition of what we will not build. That clarity is what lets us move fast without cutting corners.

phasedProcess.title: A clear, phased process
phasedProcess.description: The same backbone for every engagement - calibrated to your timeline and risk tolerance.
phasedProcess.footer: These phases are a framework, not a rigid contract. We adjust the intensity of each phase - never the underlying discipline.

standards.title: Benchmarks we ship against.
standards.description: Performance, accessibility, and code quality aren't add-ons. We define concrete targets before development starts and verify them before anything goes live.

performance.label: Performance
performance.description: Lighthouse > 90, fast initial load, lean JS bundles tuned for real devices - not lab conditions.

accessibility.label: Accessibility
accessibility.description: WCAG-aligned interfaces, full keyboard navigation, and contrast ratios that pass audit - not visual inspection.

codeQuality.label: Code Quality
codeQuality.description: Typed, reviewed, tested, and documented code that your next engineer can understand and extend without a walkthrough.

cta.title: See how this maps to your project.
cta.description: We'll walk through how these phases and standards apply to your scope - before you commit to anything.

1.title: 1. Information We Collect
1.description: When you use our contact form or project estimator, we collect the following information:
1.projectValue: Service interest, project timeline, project type, complexity level
1.techValue: IP address (for security and rate limiting purposes only), browser type

2.title: 2. How We Use Your Information
2.description: We use the information we collect to:
2.item1: Respond to your inquiries and provide project estimates
2.item3: Improve our services and website experience
2.item4: Protect against spam and abuse (rate limiting, honeypot detection)

3.title: 3. Data Storage & Security
3.description: Your data is stored securely using industry-standard encryption. We use PostgreSQL databases with SSL encryption for all data in transit and at rest. We do not sell, trade, or transfer your personal information to third parties.

4.title: 4. Your Rights
4.item1: Request access to your personal data
4.item2: Request correction or deletion of your data
4.item4: Lodge a complaint with a data protection authority

5.title: 5. Cookies & Analytics
5.description: We may use essential cookies for site functionality. If we implement analytics tracking, we will update this policy and provide appropriate consent mechanisms.

6.title: 6. Contact Us
6.description: If you have questions about this privacy policy or your personal data, please contact us at

1.title: 1. Services
1.description: Altruvex provides web development, interface design, UI/UX design, and related digital services. All project scopes, deliverables, and timelines are agreed upon in individual project proposals.

2.title: 2. Project Estimates
2.description: Estimates provided through our online estimator tool are preliminary and non-binding. Final pricing and timelines are determined after a detailed requirements discussion and formalized in a project proposal.

3.title: 3. Intellectual Property
3.description: Upon full payment, clients receive ownership of all custom-built deliverables as specified in the project agreement. Altruvex retains the right to use general methodologies, frameworks, and non-proprietary code in future projects.

4.title: 4. Payment Terms
4.description: Payment terms are outlined in individual project proposals. Standard terms include a deposit upon project commencement with milestone-based payments throughout the project lifecycle.

5.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
5.description: Altruvex provides services on an "as-is" basis. We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from the use of our services or website.

6.title: 6. Modifications
6.description: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date.

7.title: 7. Contact
7.description: For questions about these terms, please contact us at

exitIntent.subtitle: Wait, before you go
exitIntent.title: Let's stress-test your idea.
exitIntent.description: Not sure if we're a fit? A 30-minute technical discovery call is enough to know - no pitch, no pressure.
exitIntent.phoneError: Please enter a valid Egyptian phone number
exitIntent.phoneHint: We'll only call to coordinate the time.
exitIntent.successDescription: We've received your number. We'll reach out within 2 business hours to coordinate our call.
